definition: Ignorant wanting directed toward ending, annihilating, or escaping a mode of becoming.
notes: The project prefers ignorant wanting for non-becoming so the compound inherits the house rule for taṇhā while staying explicit about vibhava. Craving for annihilation and craving for non-becoming remain acceptable alternates when explanatory prose needs stronger continuity with familiar Buddhist-English terminology.

context_rules.context: In explanatory prose emphasizing the annihilating impulse

example_phrases.notes: One of the classic threefold forms of craving.
